query_results.generated_sql:

    ROUND(op.days_since_prior_order::numeric, 2) AS avg_days_between_orders
FROM 
    orders op
WHERE 
    op.days_since_prior_order IS NOT NULL
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: What are the top 10 products in the produce department?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  p.product_name, 
  COUNT(op.order_id) AS times_ordered
FROM 
  order_products__prior op
  JOIN products p ON op.product_id = p.product_id
  JOIN departments d ON p.department_id = d.department_id
WHERE 
  d.department ILIKE 'produce'
GROUP BY 
  p.product_name
ORDER BY 
  times_ordered DESC
LIMIT 10;

query_results.question: How many items were ordered from the yogurt aisle?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(op.order_id) 
FROM order_products__prior op
JOIN products p ON op.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN aisles a ON p.aisle_id = a.aisle_id
WHERE a.aisle ILIKE 'yogurt'
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: Which department has the most orders?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  d.department_id, 
  d.department, 
  COUNT(op.order_id) AS total_orders
FROM 
  order_products__prior op
JOIN 
  products p ON op.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN 
  departments d ON p.department_id = d.department_id
GROUP BY 
  d.department_id, 
  d.department
ORDER BY 
  total_orders DESC
LIMIT 1
query_results.reason: Expected column 'items_ordered' not found in results

query_results.question: What is the reorder rate for the dairy eggs department?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(op.reordered) / COUNT(*), 2) AS reorder_rate_pct
FROM 
  order_products__prior op
JOIN 
  products p ON op.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN 
  departments d ON p.department_id = d.department_id
WHERE 
  d.department ILIKE 'dairy eggs'
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: What is the maximum number of orders placed by a single user?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT MAX(op.user_id_count) 
FROM (
  SELECT o.user_id, COUNT(o.order_id) AS user_id_count
  FROM orders o
  GROUP BY o.user_id
) op
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: What percentage of users have placed more than 10 orders?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN o.order_number > 10 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS percentage_of_users
FROM 
  orders o
GROUP BY 
  o.user_id
HAVING 
  COUNT(o.order_id) > 10
LIMIT 20;
query_results.reason: Expected column 'pct_high_frequency' not found in results

query_results.question: What is the maximum basket size ever recorded?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT MAX(op.order_id_count) AS max_basket_size
FROM (
  SELECT COUNT(*) AS order_id_count
  FROM (
    SELECT order_id
    FROM order_products__prior
    UNION ALL
    SELECT order_id
    FROM order_products__train
  ) AS all_orders
  GROUP BY order_id
) op
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: What is the average add to cart order position across all orders?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  ROUND(AVG(op.add_to_cart_order::numeric), 2) AS average_add_to_cart_order
FROM 
  orders o
  JOIN (
    SELECT 
      order_id, 
      add_to_cart_order
    FROM 
      order_products__prior
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
      order_id, 
      add_to_cart_order
    FROM 
      order_products__train
  ) op ON o.order_id = op.order_id
LIMIT 20

query_results.question: What fraction of all items ordered are reorders?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(op.reordered)::numeric / COUNT(*), 2) AS reorder_rate_pct
FROM 
  (SELECT * FROM order_products__prior UNION ALL SELECT * FROM order_products__train) op
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: Which aisle has the highest reorder rate?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  a.aisle,
  COUNT(*) AS total_items,
  SUM(op.reordered) AS reordered_items,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(op.reordered) / COUNT(*), 2) AS reorder_rate_pct
FROM 
  order_products__prior op
  JOIN products p ON op.product_id = p.product_id
  JOIN aisles a ON p.aisle_id = a.aisle_id
GROUP BY 
  a.aisle
ORDER BY 
  reorder_rate_pct DESC
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: What is the average days between orders for users with more than 5 orders?
query_results.generated_sql:
WITH user_orders AS (
  SELECT 
    o.user_id,
    o.order_number,
    o.days_since_prior_order
  FROM 
    orders o
  WHERE 
    o.eval_set = 'prior'
),
user_order_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    uo.user_id,
    AVG(uo.days_since_prior_order) AS avg_days_between_orders,
    COUNT(uo.order_number) AS total_orders
  FROM 
    user_orders uo
  GROUP BY 
    uo.user_id
)
SELECT 
  ROUND(uos.avg_days_between_orders::numeric, 2) AS avg_days_between_orders
FROM 
  user_order_stats uos
WHERE 
  uos.total_orders > 5
LIMIT 20

query_results.question: How many orders are first time orders from new customers?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  o.order_dow,
  COUNT(o.order_id) AS total_first_time_orders
FROM 
  orders o
WHERE 
  o.order_number = 1
GROUP BY 
  o.order_dow
LIMIT 20

query_results.question: What is the average basket size for first time orders versus repeat orders?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  o.order_number,
  ROUND(AVG(op.items)::numeric, 2) AS avg_basket_size
FROM 
  orders o
JOIN 
  (
    SELECT 
      order_id, 
      COUNT(*) AS items
    FROM 
      (
        SELECT 
          order_id, 
          product_id
        FROM 
          order_products__prior
        UNION ALL
        SELECT 
          order_id, 
          product_id
        FROM 
          order_products__train
      ) op
    GROUP BY 
      order_id
  ) op ON o.order_id = op.order_id
GROUP BY 
  o.order_number
ORDER BY 
  o.order_number
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: Show customer segments by order frequency as low medium and high with user counts
query_results.generated_sql:
WITH user_order_counts AS (
  SELECT o.user_id, COUNT(*) AS total_orders
  FROM orders o
  GROUP BY o.user_id
)
SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN u.total_orders <= 3  THEN 'Low (1-3 orders)'
    WHEN u.total_orders <= 10 THEN 'Medium (4-10 orders)'
    ELSE 'High (10+ orders)'
  END AS segment,
  COUNT(*) AS user_count,
  ROUND(AVG(u.total_orders)::numeric, 2) AS avg_orders
FROM user_order_counts u
GROUP BY 
  CASE 
    WHEN u.total_orders <= 3  THEN 'Low (1-3 orders)'
    WHEN u.total_orders <= 10 THEN 'Medium (4-10 orders)'
    ELSE 'High (10+ orders)'
  END
ORDER BY segment
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: Which products are most often bought together with organic strawberries?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  p2.product_name, 
  COUNT(DISTINCT op1.order_id) AS co_purchase_count
FROM 
  order_products__prior op1
  JOIN order_products__prior op2 ON op1.order_id = op2.order_id
  JOIN products p1 ON op1.product_id = p1.product_id
  JOIN products p2 ON op2.product_id = p2.product_id
WHERE 
  p1.product_name ILIKE 'organic strawberries'
  AND p2.product_name NOT ILIKE 'organic strawberries'
GROUP BY 
  p2.product_name
ORDER BY 
  co_purchase_count DESC
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: Which aisles are most popular in the morning between 6am and 11am?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  a.aisle,
  SUM(CASE WHEN o.order_hour_of_day BETWEEN 6 AND 11 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS morning_orders
FROM 
  order_products__prior op
JOIN 
  orders o ON op.order_id = o.order_id
JOIN 
  products p ON op.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN 
  aisles a ON p.aisle_id = a.aisle_id
GROUP BY 
  a.aisle
ORDER BY 
  morning_orders DESC
LIMIT 20

query_results.question: What is the average basket size per department ordered by highest first?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  d.department,
  ROUND(AVG(basket_size.items)::numeric, 2) AS avg_basket_size
FROM 
  (
    SELECT 
      op.order_id,
      p.department_id,
      COUNT(DISTINCT op.product_id) AS items
    FROM 
      order_products__prior op
    JOIN 
      products p ON op.product_id = p.product_id
    GROUP BY 
      op.order_id, p.department_id
  ) basket
JOIN 
  departments d ON basket.department_id = d.department_id
GROUP BY 
  d.department
ORDER BY 
  avg_basket_size DESC
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: Which departments have the highest reorder rates ordered by rate descending?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  d.department,
  COUNT(*) AS total_items,
  
  SUM(op.reordered) AS reordered_items,
  
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(op.reordered) / COUNT(*), 2) AS reorder_rate_pct
FROM 
  order_products__prior op
JOIN 
  products p ON op.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN 
  departments d ON p.department_id = d.department_id
GROUP BY 
  d.department
ORDER BY 
  reorder_rate_pct DESC
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: Show the top 10 healthiest most ordered products with nutrition grade a or b
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  p.product_name,
  COUNT(*) AS times_ordered,
  pe.nutrition_grade_fr,
  ROUND(pe.energy_100g::numeric, 2) AS energy_100g,
  ROUND(pe.proteins_100g::numeric, 2) AS proteins_100g,
  ROUND(pe.sugars_100g::numeric, 2) AS sugars_100g
FROM 
  order_products__prior op
JOIN 
  products p ON op.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN 
  product_enriched pe ON LOWER(p.product_name) = LOWER(pe.product_name)
WHERE 
  pe.nutrition_grade_fr ILIKE 'a' OR pe.nutrition_grade_fr ILIKE 'b'
GROUP BY 
  p.product_name, pe.nutrition_grade_fr, pe.energy_100g, pe.proteins_100g, pe.sugars_100g
ORDER BY 
  COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 10;

query_results.question: Which hour of the day has the highest average basket size?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  o.order_hour_of_day,
  ROUND(AVG(basket_size.items::numeric), 2) AS avg_basket_size
FROM 
  orders o
JOIN 
  (
    SELECT 
      order_id, 
      COUNT(*) AS items
    FROM 
      order_products__prior
    GROUP BY 
      order_id
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
      order_id, 
      COUNT(*) AS items
    FROM 
      order_products__train
    GROUP BY 
      order_id
  ) basket_size ON o.order_id = basket_size.order_id
GROUP BY 
  o.order_hour_of_day
ORDER BY 
  avg_basket_size DESC
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: What are the top 5 products with the highest reorder rates that have been ordered more than 10000 times?
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  p.product_name, 
  ROUND(op.reordered::numeric, 2) AS reorder_rate
FROM 
  order_products__prior op
  JOIN products p ON op.product_id = p.product_id
GROUP BY 
  p.product_name, op.reordered
HAVING 
  SUM(op.reordered) > 10000
ORDER BY 
  reorder_rate DESC
LIMIT 5;

query_results.question: Show new versus returning customer order counts by day of week
query_results.generated_sql:
WITH first_orders AS (
  SELECT user_id, MIN(order_number) AS first_order_num
  FROM orders 
  GROUP BY user_id
)
SELECT 
  o.order_dow,
  COUNT(o.order_id) AS total_orders,
  SUM(CASE WHEN o.order_number = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS new_customer_orders,
  SUM(CASE WHEN o.order_number > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS returning_customer_orders
FROM 
  orders o
GROUP BY 
  o.order_dow
ORDER BY 
  o.order_dow
LIMIT 20;

query_results.question: Show me orders placed yesterday
query_results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.order_id, o.user_id, o.order_number, o.order_dow, o.order_hour_of_day, o.days_since_prior_order 
FROM orders o 
WHERE o.order_id IN (SELECT op.order_id FROM order_products__prior op UNION ALL SELECT op.order_id FROM order_products__train op) 
AND o.order_dow = (EXTRACT(DOW FROM CURRENT_DATE) - 1) % 7 
AND o.order_hour_of_day = (EXTRACT(HOUR FROM CURRENT_TIME) - 1) % 24 
LIMIT 20
